title: Article about the tools used in the making of Rebuild
display_title: Article about the tools used in the making of Rebuild
wikitext:
[]: Article about the tools used in the making of Rebuild             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Nov 09, 2009 7:14 am http://journal.mycom.co.jp/articles/2009/11/09/eva3dcg/index.html Anything interesting in there? I can make out "Autodesk 3ds Max" for the CG, and some stuff about shaders.

[]:             Quote Post by EdZ » Mon Nov 09, 2009 8:07 am From what I can tell from the (somewhat suspect) google translation, they worked out some of Unit 05's leg movements by playing with some of the prototype Revoltechs.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Mon Nov 09, 2009 2:13 pm EdZ wrote: From what I can tell from the (somewhat suspect) google translation, they worked out some of Unit 05's leg movements by playing with some of the prototype Revoltechs.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Nov 09, 2009 6:23 pm ( Source ) Whuzzis say? Edited by Staff to prevent hot/direct linking.

[]:             Quote Post by Helius » Mon Nov 09, 2009 6:44 pm No. of admittance: 2,659,531 Revenue: 3,633,937,570 (yen?) (Up to 7th Sept)

[]:             Quote Post by Kanchan » Tue Nov 10, 2009 6:04 pm They used bunch of shader plug-ins.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Aug 04, 2014 2:52 pm Five-year bump. So it doesn't look like this has been talked about much, but the question of software came up on r/evangelion, which prompted me to scrub through the credits for all three films and this is what I gathered: The Eva Monkey wrote: According to the credits for both 1.0 and 2.0, they used Autodesk. Couldn't spot the name in 3.0, but they did cite PSOFT, which makes 3ds Max Plugins, so it would seem they've been using Autodesk 3ds Max on the films. Also, while researching, I found that Wikipedia claims Lightwave was used in some amount on The End of Evangelion. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LightWave_3D#Anime_and_Japanese_OVA_Series_which_were_created_with_the_help_of_LightWave
